talk_metadata.talk_title: neoadjuvant pembrolizumab stratified by tumor mutation burden and high-risk stage two, three deficient mismatch repair or MSI-high colorectal cancer. The NeoPRISM-CRC study
rhetorical_signals: proposed that in the super molecular responders, these patients could be considered for non-operative management
propose that this could possibly be used as a tool for real-time clinical decision making in the future

crispro_opportunity.description: Integration of baseline TCR clonal expansion and dynamic ctDNA clearance to predict pCR in MMRd CRC.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: dynamic ctDNA clearance adds a strong discrimination layer beyond TCR alone, separating patients with similar immune profiles into distinct confidence regions of achieving response
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO's computational spine can integrate TCR repertoire dynamics with longitudinal ctDNA kinetics to create a high-resolution predictive model for non-operative management selection.

cited_competitors.name: Personalis
cited_competitors.context: Provided the Next Personal assay for ctDNA profiling

unresolved_questions: Optimal immunotherapy regimen and duration for MMRd colorectal cancers
Whether sub-clonality, copy number changes, or whole genome doubling correlate with response

talk_metadata.talk_title: early findings from MONITOR-BREAST, ctDNA dynamics during neoadjuvant therapy using an ultrasensitive MRD assay
rhetorical_signals: we don't always know what a negative test means
I can't answer that question because we don't have the data

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Presence of residual disease in patients who remain ctDNA negative even with ultrasensitive testing, suggesting a limit to shedding or detection thresholds.
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: many of these patients who have residual disease detectable remain negative even with ultrasensitive testing and even with serial testing

key_data_gaps: Long-term survival outcomes are not yet available

crispro_opportunity.description: Addressing the 'false negative' gap where patients with residual disease are ctDNA negative.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: perhaps it's not the sensitivity that really matters... we don't always know what a negative test means
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Utilizing multi-omic integration (proteomics/transcriptomics) alongside ctDNA to identify non-shedding residual disease.

cited_competitors.name: I-SPY 2
cited_competitors.context: Used as a benchmark for RCB index and ctDNA outcomes

cited_competitors.name: PredictDNA
cited_competitors.context: Previous study showing outcomes for ctDNA negative patients

cited_competitors.name: NSABP B-59
cited_competitors.context: Previous study showing outcomes for ctDNA negative patients

unresolved_questions: Whether it is the pathologic response or the ctDNA that provides more critical prognostic information
Which specific time points are most critical for sampling in routine practice

talk_metadata.talk_title: Phase 2 study of adjuvant PD-1 blockade with endocrine therapy in hormone receptor-positive and inflammatory breast cancer
rhetorical_signals: It's purely conjecture, but the spatial profiling would really help tease that out

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Immunosuppressive microenvironment in IBC (myeloid cells, vascular aberrations, fibroblasts) potentially limiting PD-1 efficacy.
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: the two that had an ongoing durable disease-free survival actually had a lower maximum variant allele frequency detected in their nodal tissue
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: few patients that did progress typically had to end their treatment early due to adverse events

trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Lack of preoperative ctDNA levels to establish baseline disease burden before multimodal therapy
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Very small N for sequencing analysis (n=6)

key_data_gaps: Spatial profiling of B-T cell interactions

crispro_opportunity.description: Investigating the role of B-cell biology and IGH locus deletions in response to checkpoint blockade in IBC.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: found deletions in the area of the immunoglobulin heavy locus, really suggesting that there may be some B cell biology that's contributing to the response
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Applying computational analysis to B-cell receptor (BCR) repertoires to identify specific B-cell clones that correlate with durable response.

unresolved_questions: How deletions in the IGH locus dictate response to checkpoint blockade
The role of B-T cell interactions in the lymph node for IBC response

talk_metadata.talk_title: molecular residual disease interception in local regionally advanced head and neck squamous cell carcinoma, the MERIDIAN phase two trial
rhetorical_signals: recruitment was paused after 52 patients because only two patients were able to be enrolled into part C
I wouldn't do that [observation arm]... I am totally in favor of removing randomization

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Low sensitivity of the initial tumor-informed assay (50% sensitivity at 8-12 weeks), missing a significant portion of MRD+ patients.
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: grade two tongue pain and neck edema after three cycles of therapy

trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Low MRD prevalence (only 30% assumed, but actual was lower), leading to trial pause
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: More sensitive ctDNA assay (initially used RADAAR)
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Underpowered due to low enrollment in treatment arm (n=2)

key_data_gaps: Sufficient sample size for randomized comparison
Validation of HPV DNA vs RADAAR specificity

crispro_opportunity.description: Combining tumor-informed assays with HPV DNA sequencing to achieve 100% sensitivity in HNSCC.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: when we combine OPC patients both a tumor-informed assay and the HPV DNA, we increase sensitivity to 100%
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Developing a hybrid biomarker spine that weights tumor-informed mutations and viral DNA kinetics to optimize the sensitivity/specificity trade-off.

cited_competitors.name: Personalis
cited_competitors.context: New assay (Next Personal) being adopted for protocol amendment

unresolved_questions: Whether ctDNA detection more than a year before recurrence is possible with newer assays
How to tune the combined HPV/RADAAR assay to maintain specificity while keeping high sensitivity

talk_metadata.talk_title: unlocking endocrine resistance, co-targeting KAT6 and ER with bifetostat, PF-07248144, and fulvestrant drives tumor and ctDNA response in ER-positive, HER2-negative metastatic breast cancer
rhetorical_signals: limitations include a ctDNA analysis constrained by small sample size and limited time points

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Insufficient inhibition of ER signaling when used without fulvestrant, as evidenced by lower ctDNA clearance rates.

crispro_opportunity.description: Using early ctDNA clearance of ESR1 and PI3K pathway mutations as a surrogate for dose selection in epigenetic therapies.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: ctDNA clearance was 26.5% for the overall ctDNA and 63% for ESR1 mutation and 31% for PI3K pathway gene mutations
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Using the computational spine to map the correlation between H3K23 acetylation (PD) and specific mutation clearance to refine dose-finding in Phase 1/2.

unresolved_questions: Determinants of responder versus non-responders in the bifetostat/fulvestrant combination

talk_metadata.talk_title: ctDNA Clearance as a Prognostic Biomarker in Extensive Stage Small Cell Lung Cancer: Insights from the LUMINOUS Phase 3B Study
rhetorical_signals: we need additional datasets showing the correlation of clinical outcomes and ctDNA dynamics

key_data_gaps: Prospective randomized trials showing ctDNA-guided vs standard care

crispro_opportunity.description: Utilizing tumor-naive methylation assays for rapid monitoring in SCLC where tissue is limited.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: due to tissue limited availability, it is not easy to obtain tissue for the tumor-informed assay
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Integrating methylation-based tumor-naive assays with high-shedding SCLC biology to create a rapid-turnaround monitoring platform.

cited_competitors.name: CASPIAN study
cited_competitors.context: Used as a benchmark for OS/PFS

unresolved_questions: Clinical utility of ctDNA-guided therapy vs standard care in SCLC

talk_metadata.talk_title: ctDNA as a Prognostic and Pharmacodynamic Biomarker Following First or Later Line Treatment of Advanced Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer with BNT116 and Cemiplimab
rhetorical_signals: I think we're dealing with very small patient numbers here
I don't know that we have the samples to answer that question

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Low detection rate (24% undetectable at baseline) compared to tumor-informed assays, potentially missing low-shedding tumors.

key_data_gaps: Correlation of stable disease with ctDNA clearance

crispro_opportunity.description: Combining mRNA vaccines (BNT116) with PD-1 inhibitors and monitoring via ctDNA kinetics.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: decrease of ctDNA in patients achieving at least a 50% reduction occurs as early as cycle two
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Using the computational spine to correlate vaccine-induced T-cell expansion (TCR-seq) with early ctDNA clearance to identify 'molecular responders' before radiological response.

unresolved_questions: Whether plasma TCR sequences are complementary to PBMC TCR-seq
Correlation between stable disease and ctDNA clearance in long-term responders
